core_mechanism: A critical-threshold (tipping-point) transition in a nonlinear influence-spreading/contagion dynamics driven by a fixed fraction of immutable 'source' units, at which the dominant metastable state loses stability, causing the collective switching time to cross over from exponential in system size (activated escape over a barrier) below the threshold to logarithmic in system size above it
brief_reason: The abstract reports that once a fixed fraction of unswayable agents exceeds a critical value p_c, the time for the whole system to flip drops from T_c ~ exp(alpha(p)N) to T_c ~ ln N, which is exactly a threshold-driven loss of metastability with a barrier-crossing-to-fast-relaxation scaling crossover.
